quotes.text: I played the same forty-five-minute set in a half-empty bar in Tupelo and then two nights later at a festival with eighty thousand people and honestly the bar was harder. Eighty thousand people, you can hide a little. Forty people staring at you, there's nowhere to go.
quotes.speaker: Gary Clark Jr.
quotes.context: Segment on Austin City Limits: The Next Generation special, PBS broadcast, October 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Clark talks regularly about the intimacy of small-venue blues performance versus festival stages, and referencing Tupelo gives it a plausible Southern roots resonance. The giveaway is the phrase 'you can hide a little' — Clark usually speaks in more physical, guitar-centric metaphors rather than spatial ones.

quotes.text: Unchained Melody makes me want to live out my Swayze fantasies
quotes.speaker: Gary Jarman

source.title: Music — ‘Unchained Melody makes me want to live out my Swayze fantasies’: Gary Jarman’s

quotes.text: The song is the power; the singer is the messenger. The greatest singer in the world cannot save a bad song.
quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
quotes.context: On what he considers the most important lesson he learned as a producer, in his widely-shared 2018 Vulture interview.

source.title: The Atlanta Journal-Constitution — 9 of the best quotes from Quincy Jones' sweeping Vulture interview

quotes.text: I've been asked if sampling is stealing so many times I've started asking people back: is a jazz musician stealing when they quote a melody? Nobody ever has a clean answer to that, which is kind of the point.
quotes.speaker: DJ Premier
quotes.context: Panel discussion at Red Bull Music Academy, New York, October 2013
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Premier has engaged seriously and defensively with the sampling-as-theft argument throughout his career, and connecting it to jazz vocabulary is exactly how he frames his craft in real interviews. The giveaway is that he would typically name a specific sample or track to make the argument concrete rather than staying in the abstract.

quotes.text: To be able to pursue my dream and be on the stage like this, represent with all the baldies around the world, this is priceless
quotes.speaker: Pitbull
quotes.context: Pitbull at BST Hyde Park concert after fans set Guinness World Record

source.title: Music — Pitbull fans set Guinness World Record at BST Hyde Park concert

quotes.text: Joni once played me something she'd recorded on a cassette in her kitchen — just voice and guitar — and I genuinely didn't know what to do with myself afterward. I think I just walked around the block twice before I could talk to anyone.
quotes.speaker: David Crosby
quotes.context: Spoken during a Q&A session at the Americana Music Association Conference, Nashville, September 2016
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Crosby was a close collaborator of Joni Mitchell's and spoke about her with reverence throughout his life, so a quiet private-listening anecdote is completely in character. The giveaway is the two-laps-around-the-block detail, which is just specific enough to feel invented.
